instruction:
The Question Paper contains three sections.
 Section A has 24 questions. Attempt any 20 questions.
 Section B has 24 questions. Attempt any 20 questions.
 Section C has 12 questions. Attempt any 10 questions.
 All questions carry equal marks.
 There is no negative marking.

questions.question: Living organisms show internal growth due to the addition of materials and formation of cells inside the body. Such a method is called _______.
questions.question_raw: Living organisms show internal growth due to the addition of materials and formation of cells inside the body. Such a method is called _______.
questions.explanation: The method in which living organism show internal growth due to the addition of materials fand formation of cells inside the body is called intussusception.

questions.question: The structure of the plasma membrane fluid mosaic model is proposed by:
questions.question_raw: The structure of the plasma membrane fluid mosaic model is proposed by:
questions.explanation: The fluid mosaic model explains various observations regarding the structure of functional cell membranes. The model, which was devised by SJ Singer and GL Nicolson in 1972, describes the cell membrane as a two-dimensional liquid that restricts the lateral diffusion of membrane components.

questions.question: The sporophytes bearing sporangia having leaf-like appendages are called ________.
questions.question_raw: The sporophytes bearing sporangia having leaf-like appendages are called ________.
questions.explanation: The sporophytes bear sporangia that are subtended by leaf-like appendages called sporophylls.

questions.question:
Match the following list of animals with their level of organisation.
 Division of Labour (Column I)
 Animal (Column II)
 a. Organ level
 i. Pheretima
 b. Cellular aggregate level
 ii. Fasciola
 c. Tissue level
 iii. Spongilla
 d. Organ system level
 iv. Obelia
Choose the correct match showing division of labour with animal example.
questions.question_raw:
Match the following list of animals with their level of organisation.

Division of Labour (Column I) Animal (Column II)
a. Organ level i. Pheretima
b. Cellular aggregate level ii. Fasciola
c. Tissue level iii. Spongilla
d. Organ system level iv. Obelia

Choose the correct match showing division of labour with animal example.
questions.explanation: The cellular level of organizational in Spongilla ( phylum Porifera), tissue level in obelia(phylum Coelenterata), organ level in Fasciola ( phylum Platyhelminthes) and organ system level in Pheritima (phylum Annelida).

questions.question: The ovary belonging to a single free carpel is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The ovary belonging to a single free carpel is called ________.
questions.explanation: When more than one carpel is present, they may be free, it is called apocarpous.

questions.question: The most active phagocytic white blood cells are:
questions.question_raw: The most active phagocytic white blood cells are:
questions.explanation: The most active phagocytic white blood cells are neutrophils and monocytes. These cells try to engulf the microbes that enter the blood to cause disease.

questions.question: Nucleoplasm otherwise called as ______.
questions.question_raw: Nucleoplasm otherwise called as ______.
questions.explanation: The nuclear matrix or the nucleoplasm contains nucleolus and chromatin. The nucleoli are spherical structures present in the nucleoplasm.

questions.question: An important site for the formation of glycoproteins and glycolipids is:
questions.question_raw: An important site for the formation of glycoproteins and glycolipids is:
questions.explanation: Synthesis of glycoproteins and glycolipids take place in Golgi apparatus inside the cell. Golgi apparatus is located near the nucleus of the cell.

questions.question: The movement of ions against the concentration gradient is
questions.question_raw: The movement of ions against the concentration gradient is
questions.explanation: Active transport is the movement of molecules across a cell membrane in the direction against their concentration gradient, i.e. moving from an area of lower concentration to an area of higher concentration.

questions.question: Nucleic acids are serve as:
questions.question_raw: Nucleic acids are serve as:
questions.explanation: Nucleic acids carry hereditary information and are passed on from parental generation to progeny.

questions.question: Which is the topmost in Botany?
questions.question_raw: Which is the topmost in Botany?
questions.explanation: The hierarchical order of taxonomic categories is a sequence of categories in decreasing order is from kingdom to species. The kingdom has the highest rank followed by division, class, order, family, genus, and species in botany.

questions.question: Plants can synthesis all types of fatty acids but animals lack the machinery to synthesis three fatty acids called essential fatty acids. They are:
questions.question_raw: Plants can synthesis all types of fatty acids but animals lack the machinery to synthesis three fatty acids called essential fatty acids. They are:
questions.explanation: Plants can synthesis all types of fatty acids but animal cells lack the machinery to synthesis three fatty acids called essential fatty acids. These are Linoleic, Linolenic, and Arachidonic acids.

questions.question: In Phaeophyceae, the food stored in the form of:
questions.question_raw: In Phaeophyceae, the food stored in the form of:
questions.explanation: In Phaeophyceae, food is stored as complex carbohydrates which may be in the form of laminarin or mannitol.

questions.question: Haplontic life cycle is seen in_______.
questions.question_raw: Haplontic life cycle is seen in_______.
questions.explanation: In the haplontic life cycle, the dominant, photosynthetic phase is the free-living gametophyte which is seen in Volvox, Spirogyra, and Chlamydomonas.

questions.question: The diagram below shows
questions.question_raw: The diagram below shows
questions.explanation: If gynoecium is situated in the centre and otherparts of the flower are located on the rim of the thalamus almost at thesame level, it is called perigynous. The ovary here is said to be half inferior.

questions.question: Myelin sheath is mainly made of______.
questions.question_raw: Myelin sheath is mainly made of______.
questions.explanation: The myelin sheath is the outer layer of the axon of nerve cells. It is made up of phospholipids. It is mainly protective in nature, acts as an insulator, and helps in the transmission of impulses.

questions.question: The site for the light reactions is ________.
questions.question_raw: The site for the light reactions is ________.
questions.explanation: In-plant cells, the chloroplasts are responsible for trapping light energy essential for photosynthesis. The grana, in the plastid, is the site of light reactions.

questions.question: The nucleotide chemical components are:
questions.question_raw: The nucleotide chemical components are:
questions.explanation: The nucleotide has three chemically distinct components. One is a heterocyclic compound, the second is a monosaccharide and the third a phosphoric acid or phosphate.

questions.question: What contains the actual account of habitat and distribution of plants of a given area?
questions.question_raw: What contains the actual account of habitat and distribution of plants of a given area?
questions.explanation: Manuals are useful in providing information for the identification of names of species found in an area. Monographs contain information on anyone taxon. Catalogue is also another means of recording descriptions which helps incorrect identification. Hence, the correct option is Flora.

questions.question: The pericardial space in cockroach is regularly altered by the muscles:
questions.question_raw: The pericardial space in cockroach is regularly altered by the muscles:
questions.explanation: Cockroaches have an open circulatory system. Their pericardial space is regularly altered by alary muscles.

questions.question: In which one of the following, male and female gametophytes do not have free living independent existence?
questions.question_raw: In which one of the following, male and female gametophytes do not have free living independent existence?
questions.explanation: In Cedrus plants, male and female gametophytes do not have a free independent existence. They remain attached to sporophytic forms to obtain nutrients.

questions.question:
Which one is correct?
 Sea Lily is an echinoderm.
 Blue-green algae are a kind of fungus.
 Maidenhair tree is an angiosperm.
 Sea horse is related to dolphins.
questions.question_raw:
Which one is correct?

  1. Sea Lily is an echinoderm.
  2. Blue-green algae are a kind of fungus.
  3. Maidenhair tree is an angiosperm.
  4. Sea horse is related to dolphins.
questions.explanation: Sea Lily is an echinoderm having spine all over the body. Blue-green algae is a class of bacteria. Maidenhair tree is pteridophytes and the sea horse is not related to the dolphin.

questions.question:
Match the following and choose the correct option:
 Column I 
 Column II
 a. Aleurone layer
 i. Without fertilization
 b. Parthenocarpic fruit
 ii. Nutrition
 c. Ovule
 iii. Double fertilization
 d. Endosperm
 iv. Seed
questions.question_raw:
Match the following and choose the correct option:

Column I Column II
a. Aleurone layer i. Without fertilization
b. Parthenocarpic fruit ii. Nutrition
c. Ovule iii. Double fertilization
d. Endosperm iv. Seed
questions.explanation: Aleurone layer is nutritious, parthenocarpic fruit is formed without fertilization, the ovule develops into a seed, and endosperm is formed during double fertilization where one sperm fuses with a secondary nucleus.

questions.question: The life span of RBC and WBC are respectively:
questions.question_raw: The life span of RBC and WBC are respectively:
questions.explanation: Red blood cells (RBC) live for 120 days and white blood cells (WBC) live for 90 days. Afterwards, they are degraded in the spleen.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Coenzymes are also called prosthetic groups.
Reason (R): Coenzymes and prosthetic groups are cofactors.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): Coenzymes are also called prosthetic groups.
Reason (R): Coenzymes and prosthetic groups are cofactors.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is false but R is true.

correct_option_texts: A is false but R is true.
questions.explanation: Cofactor may be inorganic or organic in nature. Organic cofactors are of two types, coenzymes and prosthetic groups. Coenzymes are easily separable nonprotein organic cofactors. Prosthetic groups are nonprotein organic cofactors firmly attached to apoenzymes (protein part of enzyme).

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Living systems have a high degree of tendency for undergoing entropy.
Reason (R): Living systems are unable to overcome entropy.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): Living systems have a high degree of tendency for undergoing entropy.
Reason (R): Living systems are unable to overcome entropy.

options.text: A is true but R is false.
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
A is false but R is true.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.

correct_option_texts: A is true but R is false.
questions.explanation: Law of entropy states when a living or non living system is left to itself, then it tends to increase its disorder or randomness i.e. entropy. So entropy is the degree of unavailable or useless energy. Living systems carry a number of chemical reactions and physical changes. Since no energy transfer or transformation is cent percent efficient, there is a regular loss of energy, thus bringing entropy. Living systems overcome entropy by continuous input of usable or free energy. The Iiving organism obtain this required free energy either from radiant energy by photosynthesis (eg. plants) or from ready made food (eg. animals).

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Living organisms possess specific individuality with a definite shape and size.
Reason (R): Both living and nonliving entities resemble each other at the lower level of organization.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): Living organisms possess specific individuality with a definite shape and size.
Reason (R): Both living and nonliving entities resemble each other at the lower level of organization.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
A is false but R is true.
A is true but R is false.
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.

correct_option_texts: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
questions.explanation: All living organisms have definite shape and size and all show specific individuality with an orderly mannered organisation whereas at the lower level of organisation, both the living and non living are made up of atoms.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Embryo contains epicotyl and hypocotyl parts.
Reason (R): Epicotyl forms shoot and hypocotyl forms root.
questions.question_raw:
Assertion (A): Embryo contains epicotyl and hypocotyl parts.
Reason (R): Epicotyl forms shoot and hypocotyl forms root.

options.text: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is true but R is false.
A is false but R is true.
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.

correct_option_texts: A is true but R is false.
questions.explanation: The embryo has an axis and one or two cotyledons. One end of embryo axis possesses plumule or future shoot. The portion of the embryo axis lying between the cotyledons and the plumule is called epicotyl while the one between cotyledons and the radicle is termed as hypocotyl. The seed is a fertilized and ripened ovule. Seed has an embryo, a food reserve and protective covering called seed coat.

questions.question: Animals of class Mammalia have:
questions.question_raw: Animals of class Mammalia have:
questions.explanation: The animals of class Mammalia have seven cervical vertebrae and a four-chambered heart. Mammalian is characterized by the presence of mammary glands to feed their young ones.

questions.question: Roots develop from which part of seed?
questions.question_raw: Roots develop from which part of seed?
questions.explanation: The radicle is the first part of the seedling to emerge from the seed. It will develop into the primary root from which root hairs and lateral roots will develop.

questions.question: Platelets are formed from the________.
questions.question_raw: Platelets are formed from the________.
questions.explanation: Platelets are a little piece of blood cells that help wound healing and prevent bleeding by forming a blood clot. It is formed from the megakaryocytes.

questions.question: Proteins carry out many functions in living organisms such as:
questions.question_raw: Proteins carry out many functions in living organisms such as:

options.text: Role in catalytic activity of the enzyme

questions.explanation: Proteins are molecules made of amino acids. They also play a central role in biological processes. For example, transport molecules such as oxygen, keep us healthy as part of the immune system and transmit messages from cell to cell.

questions.question: Each statement in the key is called ______.
questions.question_raw: Each statement in the key is called ______.
questions.explanation: The keys are based on the contrasting characters generally in a pair called a couplet. Manuals are useful in providing information for identification of names of species found in an area. Monographs contain information on anyone taxon. Hence, the correct option is Lead.

questions.question: The organelle involved in respiration is_______.
questions.question_raw: The organelle involved in respiration is_______.
questions.explanation: Mitochondria are the sites of aerobic respiration. They produce cellular energy in the form of ATP.

questions.question: Half of the total carbon dioxide fixation on earth is carried out by algae through ________.
questions.question_raw: Half of the total carbon dioxide fixation on earth is carried out by algae through ________.
questions.explanation: Being photosynthetic, algae increase the level of dissolved oxygen in their immediate environment.

questions.question: Locomotory organ of Annelida is:
questions.question_raw: Locomotory organ of Annelida is:
questions.explanation: Locomotory organ of annelia is parapodia. Parapodium are in form of lateral fleshy protrusions on the body surface that help in movement and capturing food.

questions.question: In which type of placentation, the ovary is unilocular with a single ovule?
questions.question_raw: In which type of placentation, the ovary is unilocular with a single ovule?
questions.explanation: In basal placentation, the placenta develops at the base of the ovary and a single ovule is attached to it.

questions.question: Nasal septum contains:
questions.question_raw: Nasal septum contains:
questions.explanation: The nasal septum of human beings consists of hyaline cartilage which is highly elastic having collagen. It contains no nerves or blood vessels and is pearly bluish in colour.

questions.question: Cell organelles with a single membrane are ________.
questions.question_raw: Cell organelles with a single membrane are ________.
questions.explanation: Lysosomes are single membrane structures containing enzymes for digestion of all types of macromolecules.

questions.question: DNA differs from RNA in having:
questions.question_raw: DNA differs from RNA in having:
questions.explanation: DNA differs from RNA in having Thymine. Thymine is absent in RNA. RNA contains uracil in place of thymine.

questions.question: Plant and animal specimens may also be preserved as dry specimens in________.
questions.question_raw: Plant and animal specimens may also be preserved as dry specimens in________.
questions.explanation: Plant and animal specimens may also be preserved as dry specimens in museums. Hence, the correct option is the Museum.

questions.question: The locomotory organ of Annelida is__________.
questions.question_raw: The locomotory organ of Annelida is__________.
questions.explanation: The locomotory organ of Annelida is parapodia. Parapodium is in the form of lateral fleshy protrusions on the body surface that help in movement and capturing food.

questions.question: The clitellum in earthworm occur in:
questions.question_raw: The clitellum in earthworm occur in:
questions.explanation: In a mature worm, segments 14-16 are covered by a prominent dark band of glandular tissue called the clitellum.

questions.question: Animals that have become extinct in India is _____.
questions.question_raw: Animals that have become extinct in India is _____.
questions.explanation: The animals that were present earlier but now not available are called extinct animals. In India, a number of animals have become extinct such as Cheetah.

questions.question: Plastid differs from mitochondria on the basis of one of the following features. Mark the right answer.
questions.question_raw: Plastid differs from mitochondria on the basis of one of the following features. Mark the right answer.

options.text: Presence of two layers of membrane

questions.explanation: Presence of ovoid sacs, thylakoids which are in piles, called grana.

questions.question: Which of the following shows no segregation of plant parts?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following shows no segregation of plant parts?
questions.explanation: Algae are chlorophyll-bearing, simple, thalloid, autotrophic plants with the simplest body organization. Their body is thallus like without true root, stem, and leaf differentiation.

questions.question: Which group of chordates possesses sucking and circular mouth without jaws?
questions.question_raw: Which group of chordates possesses sucking and circular mouth without jaws?
questions.explanation: The organism beloging to class Cyclostomes have a sucking and circular mouth without jaws.They are primitive vertebrates. The cyclostomes are marine or fresh-water vertebrates.

questions.question: Which of the following structure is Ectodermal in origin in the mouthparts of cockroach shown in the figure below?
questions.question_raw: Which of the following structure is Ectodermal in origin in the mouthparts of cockroach shown in the figure below?
questions.explanation: The maxilla is a paired structure present in the mouth part of cockroaches. It is derived from ectoderm and is used to hold and manipulate food.

questions.question:
Instructions for Question 49 to 54:
Anita is very curious to know the reason why the virus is placed in any of the classifications. She has asked the teacher. The teacher told her that is not considered truly ‘living’. Viruses are non-cellular organisms that are characterized by having an inert crystalline structure outside the living cell. Once they infect a cell they take over the machinery of the host cell to replicate themselves, killing the host. Dmitri Ivanowsky (1892) recognized certain microbes as causal organism of the mosaic disease of tobacco.
Difference between Virus and Viroids
questions.question_raw:
Instructions for Question 49 to 54:

Anita is very curious to know the reason why the virus is placed in any of the classifications. She has asked the teacher. The teacher told her that is not considered truly ‘living’. Viruses are non-cellular organisms that are characterized by having an inert crystalline structure outside the living cell. Once they infect a cell they take over the machinery of the host cell to replicate themselves, killing the host. Dmitri Ivanowsky (1892) recognized certain microbes as causal organism of the mosaic disease of tobacco.


Difference between Virus and Viroids

options.text: Presence of high molecular weight RNA in the virus but absent in viroids
Presence of low molecular weight RNA in the virus but absent in viroids
Both absence of protein coat in viroid but present in the virus and presence of low molecular weight RNA in the virus but absent in viroids
Absence of protein coat in viroid but present in the virus

correct_option_texts: Absence of protein coat in viroid but present in the virus
questions.explanation: Absence of protein coat in viroid but present in the virus

Once they infect a cell they take over the machinery of the host cell to replicate themselves, killing the host. Which of the following is not a viral disease?
questions.explanation: Tuberculosis

correct_option_texts: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.

questions.question: The form of the thalamus in which the ovary is at top and stamens, petals, and sepals are borne below is called ________.
questions.question_raw: The form of the thalamus in which the ovary is at top and stamens, petals, and sepals are borne below is called ________.
questions.explanation: In the hypogynous flower, the gynoecium occupies the highest position while the other parts are situated below it. The ovary in such flowers is said to be superior.

questions.question: The mammary gland is an example of:
questions.question_raw: The mammary gland is an example of:
questions.explanation: Apocrine glands are a kind of exocrine gland found in the skin, breast, eyelids, and ears. Mammary glands are present in the breast that release milk in mammals.

questions.question:
Which of the following is not correct?
 Robert Brown discovered the cell.
 Schleiden and Schwann formulated the cell theory.
 Virchow explained that cells are formed from pre-existing cells.
 A unicellular organism carries out its life activities within a single cell.
questions.question_raw:
Which of the following is not correct?

  1. Robert Brown discovered the cell.
  2. Schleiden and Schwann formulated the cell theory.
  3. Virchow explained that cells are formed from pre-existing cells.
  4. A unicellular organism carries out its life activities within a single cell.
questions.explanation: Scottish botanist Robert Brown (1773-1858) was responsible for discovering the nucleus of a cell. The cell was first discovered by Robert Hooke in 1665 using a microscope.

questions.question: The following ratio is generally constant for a given species:
questions.question_raw: The following ratio is generally constant for a given species:
questions.explanation: The ratio of the sum of guanine and cytosine to the sum of adenine and thymine is always constant for a species. This is due to the formation of hydrogen bonds between purine and pyrimidine.

questions.question: Largest organelles in the cell are:
questions.question_raw: Largest organelles in the cell are:

options.text: Centrioles in animals and plastids in plant cell
Mitochondria in animal cell and chloroplast in plant cell

correct_option_texts: Mitochondria in animal cell and chloroplast in plant cell
questions.explanation: Mitochondria is the largest cell organelles in animal cell and chloroplast is the largest cell organelles in a plant cell.

questions.question: Which one is the neutral amino acid?
questions.question_raw: Which one is the neutral amino acid?
questions.explanation: Sidechains that have pure hydrocarbon alkyl groups (alkane branches) or aromatic (benzene rings) are non-polar. Examples include valine, alanine, leucine, isoleucine, phenylalanine.
